events.title: Health workers battle with few resources on DR Congo's Ebola front line
events.description: Squeezed onto the back of a motorcycle, clearly exhausted, a young woman arrived at Rwampara hospital, where overwhelmed health workers have found themselves on the front line of a deadly Ebola outbreak. The town in the violence-hit east of the Democratic Republic of Congo is one of the centres of a recent flare-up in cases of the highly contagious disease. Since the current outbreak was declared on May 15, more than 220 suspected deaths and 900 suspected cases have been identified, according to official figures, in what the World Health Organization has declared an international emergency.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Health workers battle with few resources on DR Congo's Ebola front line

events.title: World News in Brief: Sudan and Haiti updates, Afghan women’s rights
events.description: The United Nations is raising alarm over continued drone attacks in Sudan’s Darfur region after multiple strikes reportedly killed civilians and intensified fears for communities already trapped by the brutal conflict between rival militaries.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World News in Brief: Sudan and Haiti updates, Afghan women’s rights

events.title: WHO chief calls for urgent Ebola action and pandemic preparedness
events.description: The recent Ebola and hantavirus outbreaks demonstrate that the world is still vulnerable to rapidly spreading infectious diseases, Tedros Ghebreyesus, the head of the World Health Organization (WHO), warned on Saturday at the close of the 79th World Health Assembly in Geneva.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 WHO chief calls for urgent Ebola action and pandemic preparedness

events.title: International airlines urged to stick to safety measures in wake of Ebola outbreak
events.description: As a deadly Ebola strain continues to spread in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), with cases confirmed in neighbouring Uganda, the UN aviation agency is urging governments and flight operators to closely follow guidelines put in place following the COVID-19 pandemic.
events.ticker_label: ✈️ International airlines urged to stick to safety measures in wake of Ebola outbreak

events.title: WHO chief calls for DRC ceasefire to tackle Ebola outbreak
events.description: Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus warns of ‘catastrophic collision of disease and conflict’, as Uganda closes border with DRC The head of the World Health Organization has called for an immediate ceasefire in the eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo to help tackle the Ebola outbreak there, as Uganda closed its border with its neighbour in an effort to stop the spread. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus posted on social media that the region was in the midst of a “catastrophic collision of disease and conflict with the Ebola outbreak in Ituri province outpacing the response”. Tedros said on Monday that he would travel to the DRC this week. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 WHO chief calls for DRC ceasefire to tackle Ebola outbreak

events.title: Iran war: Oil shortages threaten global energy security
events.description: Strategic petroleum reserves are declining rapidly as countries release emergency crude supplies to cushion the economic impact of the Middle East conflict.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war: Oil shortages threaten global energy security

events.title: Ebola outbreak in DR Congo collides with conflict and hunger, WHO warns
events.description: The UN World Health Organization (WHO) on Wednesday warned that eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo faces a “catastrophic collision of disease and conflict” as a fast-spreading Ebola outbreak outpaces containment efforts in a region already battered by armed violence, mass displacement and acute hunger.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ebola outbreak in DR Congo collides with conflict and hunger, WHO warns

events.title: Right to strike protected under key labour treaty, says UN World Court
events.description: The UN International Court of Justice (ICJ) on Thursday ruled that the right to strike is protected under a core International Labour Organization (ILO) convention, in a landmark advisory opinion settling a long-running dispute between workers and employers worldwide.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Right to strike protected under key labour treaty, says UN World Court

events.title: Five people found alive after week trapped in flooded Laos cave
events.description: The search is continuing for a further two villagers who are still missing, rescuers say.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Five people found alive after week trapped in flooded Laos cave

events.title: Five villagers found alive in Laos cave as search continues for two missing
events.description: Video appears to show divers discovering group sitting on a rock surrounded by flood water Rescuers have reached five of seven people who have been trapped for a week in a flooded, remote cave in Laos, after days spent navigating narrow, inundated passageways amid persistent rain. Video footage shared on social media by rescue divers showed the five men crouched together on a rocky ledge in a dark cave chamber, surrounded by muddy water. “There’s no need to cry,” one of the rescue team told the men, who first entered the cave on Wednesday. Two men remain unaccounted for. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Five villagers found alive in Laos cave as search continues for two missing

events.title: Wars and geopolitical divisions constitute ‘dangerous erosion’ of  world order, warns UN chief
events.description: The UN Charter is facing one of its gravest tests in decades, Secretary-General António Guterres told the Security Council on Tuesday, warning that wars, arms races, climate shocks and the erosion of international law are placing intense pressure on the multilateral system established to prevent a third world war.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Wars and geopolitical divisions constitute ‘dangerous erosion’ of  world order, warns UN chief

events.title: Security Council LIVE: Founding principles of the UN facing ‘profound strain’, Guterres warns
events.description: The Security Council is meeting for a high-level debate on strengthening the UN-centred international system amid mounting pressure on multilateralism and deepening geopolitical divisions. Discussions are expected to focus on defending the UN Charter, reforming global governance and restoring confidence in the Security Council’s ability to respond to international crises.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Security Council LIVE: Founding principles of the UN facing ‘profound strain’, Guterres warns

events.title: One killed and nine missing after chemical explosion at US paper mill
events.description: Nine others were injured and another nine are missing after a tank ruptured at a paper mill in Longview, Washington.

events.title: Venezuela inmates occupy prison roof and set fire to mattresses to highlight alleged abuse
events.description: Inmates at Barinas prison allege they were peacefully protesting when prison staff opened fire, leaving some wounded Inmates at Venezuela’s western Barinas prison staged a protest on its roof on Sunday, piling flaming mattresses and calling for the removal of the facility’s director, whom they accused of overseeing guards as they shot unarmed prisoners. “We want justice. They are shooting us, the guards and the wardens,” a prisoner said in a video shared by the Venezuelan Observatory of Prisons, a local NGO, on X, in which a man is seen with a bullet wound in his chest. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Venezuela inmates occupy prison roof and set fire to mattresses to highlight alleged abuse

events.title: Spread of Ebola in DRC ‘outpacing’ response efforts, warns WHO
events.description: Director general of World Health Organization urges neighbouring countries to take immediate action The World Health Organization has warned that the Ebola outbreak is outpacing response efforts and countries neighbouring the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) are at high risk from the disease. “We are urgently scaling up operations, but at the moment the epidemic is outpacing us,” said the WHO’s director-general, Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, as he urged neighbouring countries to take immediate action. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Spread of Ebola in DRC ‘outpacing’ response efforts, warns WHO

events.title: Wild weather unleashes thunderstorms across south-east Australia, triggering flash flooding
events.description: Wet weather batters parts of Queensland, NSW and Tasmania amid warnings of heavy falls on Friday Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Wild weather more typical of summer than the last days of autumn has caused chaos in southeastern Australia, unleashing a string of severe thunderstorms. Heavy rainfall accompanied by 500,000 lightning strikes across Queensland and New South Wales has triggered widespread flash flooding, road closures and travel disruptions. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Wild weather unleashes thunderstorms across south-east Australia, triggering flash flooding

events.title: US strikes Iran, drawing retaliatory attack on American base
events.description: The United States struck southern Iran on Thursday, drawing retaliation from Tehran against a US military base, in the most serious clashes since an April ceasefire began. The fighting, which drew in US ally Kuwait, threatened to jettison a fragile diplomatic push to forge a peace agreement and open the Strait of Hormuz, which has become a key point of contention in efforts to formally end the war. FRANCE 24's Kethevane Gorjestani tells us more.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US strikes Iran, drawing retaliatory attack on American base

events.title: Israel strikes Tyre after declaring 'combat zones' in south Lebanon
events.description: The Israeli military said on Thursday it had begun new strikes on Hezbollah infrastructure around the southern Lebanese city of Tyre after issuing an evacuation warning to its residents. The sweeping warning – the first of its kind since an April 17 ceasefire – came as many Lebanese tried to celebrate the Muslim holiday of Eid al-Adha. FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky reports from Jerusalem.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Israel strikes Tyre after declaring 'combat zones' in south Lebanon

events.title: Israel kills chief of Hamas armed wing in Gaza strike
events.description: Israel said on Wednesday it had killed the new head of Hamas's armed wing in Gaza, Mohammed Odeh, after killing his predecessor earlier this month despite an ongoing ceasefire. Since Hamas's October 2023 attack, Israel has systematically targeted the group's leaders, both in Gaza and across the region. Gaza remains gripped by daily violence, with both the Israeli military and Hamas accusing one another of violating the truce in effect since October 10.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Israel kills chief of Hamas armed wing in Gaza strike

events.title: US ally pledges support for Trump's push to break Iran's grip on Hormuz: 'We are ready to contribute'
events.description: Czech Foreign Minister Macinka voices strong support for Trump, defends increased European defense spending and warns Iran poses a global nuclear threat.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US ally pledges support for Trump's push to break Iran's grip on Hormuz: 'We are ready to contribute'

events.title: At least 82 killed after massive gas explosion rips through coal mine in China
events.description: A massive gas explosion ripped through a coal mine in China's Shanxi province, killing at least 82 people and hospitalizing 120 others, the AP reports.

events.title: Ukraine: UN alarmed by reports of deadly strike on dormitory in occupied Luhansk
events.description: The United Nations voiced alarm on Friday over reports of an overnight attack on a vocational school and dormitory in the town of Starobilsk in Ukraine’s Luhansk region which killed and injured multiple civilians, including children.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ukraine: UN alarmed by reports of deadly strike on dormitory in occupied Luhansk

events.title: Ukraine: Russian attack destroys humanitarian food aid in Dnipro
events.description: The World Food Programme (WFP) has deplored a Russian attack on a warehouse in Dnipro, Ukraine that destroyed “a significant quantity” of food aid destined for thousands in frontline areas.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ukraine: Russian attack destroys humanitarian food aid in Dnipro

events.title: Ebola epidemic spreading rapidly and outpacing containment efforts
events.description: There are more than 900 suspected cases of the Bundibugyo strain of Ebola in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, and 220 suspected deaths, the head of the World Health Organization (WHO), Tedros Ghebreyesus, said on Monday.

events.title: Canada to order military plane fleet from Sweden in shift from US suppliers
events.description: Mark Carney announces purchase of Saab’s GlobalEye early warning aircraft to patrol Arctic territory Canada has announced plans to buy a fleet of early warning planes from Sweden’s Saab rather than a competing option from Boeing, as the country seeks to reduce reliance on US defense firms. Mark Carney, the prime minister, said on Wednesday that Canada would opt for Saab’s GlobalEye, which is based on Bombardier’s Global 6500 jet. Boeing’s E-7 Wedgetail plane – which has suffered from delays and cost overruns – had also been in contention. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Canada to order military plane fleet from Sweden in shift from US suppliers

events.title: Five people stuck in flooded cave in Laos for week found alive
events.description: Five people who were stuck in a flooded cave in central Laos for more than a week have been found alive, but two others are still missing, rescuers say.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Five people stuck in flooded cave in Laos for week found alive

events.title: US embassy in Ukraine warns of 'potentially significant air attack' that could happen in next 24 hours
events.description: The U.S. Embassy in Kyiv issued a security alert warning of a potentially significant air attack that may occur within the next 24 hours in Ukraine.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US embassy in Ukraine warns of 'potentially significant air attack' that could happen in next 24 hours

events.title: Massive Russian strike on Kyiv: ‘I hope my friends are alive’
events.description: The overnight Russian attack on Ukraine, which centred on the capital, Kyiv, reportedly involved some 90 missiles, including a powerful hypersonic ballistic missile and 60 drones. The senior UN official in the country called for an end to civilian harm.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Massive Russian strike on Kyiv: ‘I hope my friends are alive’

events.title: World Urban Forum backs ‘Baku Call to Action’ on global housing crisis
events.description: A landmark gathering of more than 57,000 participants – the largest in the history of the World Urban Forum – closed on Friday in Baku with an urgent call to rethink how the world houses its people, as a new roadmap urges governments, cities and communities to act collectively on a crisis affecting billions.

events.title: Norway will come under France's nuclear umbrella
events.description: Norway ​will open talks with France on joining its nuclear umbrella, French President Emmanuel Macron and Norwegian Prime Minister ​Jonas Gahr Stoere said on Wednesday, reflecting growing European concerns about relying on the United States for security. The move signals a shift by Norway, long a staunch Atlanticist that has relied heavily on NATO and ​the US nuclear umbrella, towards closer defence cooperation within Europe.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Norway will come under France's nuclear umbrella

events.title: Security Council LIVE: Human cost of Ukraine war, a pattern that ‘defies’ international law, says senior UN aid official
events.description: The Security Council is meeting at the request of Russia which on Friday accused Ukraine of targeting a student dormitory overnight in the occupied Luhansk region, reportedly killing six people – including children – and injuring dozens. Kyiv has denied targeting the civilian building, saying it had struck a Russian military drone command headquarters, according to news reports. Follow live coverage below and for full meetings coverage of the council, click here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Security Council LIVE: Human cost of Ukraine war, a pattern that ‘defies’ international law, says senior UN aid official

events.title: Ebola risk ‘very high’ in eastern DR Congo as UN intensifies response
events.description: The UN is rushing emergency personnel, funding and supplies into eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) to counter the fast-growing Ebola outbreak spreading through conflict-ravaged provinces.

events.title: Deadly suicide blast rips through Pakistan train route, killing at least 23
events.description: At least 23 killed and 70 wounded after a suicide bomber reportedly detonated an explosives-laden vehicle alongside an incoming passenger train in Quetta, Pakistan.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Deadly suicide blast rips through Pakistan train route, killing at least 23

events.title: What’s happening on Iran war day 90 as US launches new strikes
events.description: The US strikes near Bandar Abbas as tensions rise in the Strait of Hormuz; Iran reports no casualties or damage.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 What’s happening on Iran war day 90 as US launches new strikes

events.title: Dormitory fire at Kenyan school kills at least 15 students
events.description: Parents face anxious wait for updates after blaze tears through Utumishi girls academy in Gilgil, Nakuru county A fire that ripped through a dormitory at a girls’ school in Kenya overnight has killed at least 15 students, according to police, while dozens more were injured. Gilgil police station said at least 15 girls died at Utumishi girls academy in Gilgil, Nakuru county, about 76 miles north-east of Nairobi, according to a police report seen by Reuters. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Dormitory fire at Kenyan school kills at least 15 students

events.title: Australian police plan to form a heavily armed team in response to Bondi Beach massacre
events.description: An Australian government inquiry has heard that a state police force has worked to form a heavily armed rapid response team since the Bondi Beach shooting
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Australian police plan to form a heavily armed team in response to Bondi Beach massacre

events.title: Neymar injury threatens Brazil forward’s World Cup as scans needed on calf
events.description: Brazil's record scorer, Neymar, has not played since 2023, but injury now appears to threaten his World Cup comeback.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Neymar injury threatens Brazil forward’s World Cup as scans needed on calf

events.title: Germany news: Healthcare staff protest plan for major cuts
events.description: Medical staff in Germany are protesting about cost-saving plans that ministers says are necessary to stop spiraling costs. Meanwhile, a World War II bomb meant thousands were evacuated from their homes. DW has more.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Germany news: Healthcare staff protest plan for major cuts

events.title: 'Catastrophic collision' of Ebola and conflict, WHO head says as Uganda closes border
events.description: The eastern Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) "faces a catastrophic collision of disease and conflict" as the Ebola outbreak continues, the head of the World Health Organization (WHO) has said.

events.title: Iran and Houthi terror proxy facing Red Sea threat from pro-US African nation
events.description: Sen. Ted Cruz advocates recognizing Somaliland as the U.S. reportedly explores military access to the breakaway state's port and airbase facilities.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Iran and Houthi terror proxy facing Red Sea threat from pro-US African nation

events.title: Sixteen pupils killed in Kenya school fire
events.description: Dozens of people were also injured in the fire, the cause of which is not yet known.

events.title: Greetings from Cape Verde, where the sounds of samba, jazz and morna fill the air
events.description: Music is interwoven with the sounds of daily life in this West African island nation, which hosted two international music festivals in April and has been named the African Capital of Culture for 2028.

events.title: An exceptionally early heat wave shatters records and brings deaths in Europe
events.description: Temperature records are breaking and triggering government warnings, with London hitting 95 degrees the past two days. Experts say unpredictable and extreme weather is becoming more frequent.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 An exceptionally early heat wave shatters records and brings deaths in Europe

events.title: Kenya: Deadly fire breaks out at girls' boarding school
events.description: At least 16 students were killed and 73 injured at a girls' boarding school in central Kenya, authorities said.

events.title: Israel declares new 'combat zone' in southern Lebanon, orders evacuations
events.description: Israel expanded its military campaign in southern Lebanon on Wednesday, ordering residents to evacuate areas south of the Zahrani River as strikes intensified despite an April ceasefire. The escalation comes as Israeli officials seek to pressure Hezbollah while avoiding actions in Beirut that could complicate US-led diplomacy with Iran.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Israel declares new 'combat zone' in southern Lebanon, orders evacuations

events.title: Iran expands restrictions on distribution of news content by international media outlets
events.description: Iran is expanding restrictions on distribution of news content from the country

events.title: Kuwait says it faces a missile and drone attack as shaky ceasefire in Iran war again challenged
events.description: Kuwait says it faced a missile and drone attack as the shaky ceasefire in the Iran war was again challenged
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Kuwait says it faces a missile and drone attack as shaky ceasefire in Iran war again challenged

events.title: Chief of communications intel agency says Russia is relentlessly targeting UK
events.description: A U.K. spy chief is warning that Britain and its allies risk losing a cyber conflict against adversaries like Russia unless cybersecurity is treated with greater urgency
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Chief of communications intel agency says Russia is relentlessly targeting UK

events.title: World's humanitarian system buckling, 'no longer fit for purpose,' US-based researchers say
events.description: A new Lancet report argues the global humanitarian system needs major reform as crises around the world overwhelm current aid delivery.

events.title: Pope Leo warns AI risks becoming tool of 'domination, exclusion and death' in new encyclical
events.description: The pope unveils "Magnifica Humanitas" encyclical, warning artificial intelligence risks becoming a tool of domination unless governments set moral limits.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Pope Leo warns AI risks becoming tool of 'domination, exclusion and death' in new encyclical

events.title: The possible return of Somali pirates poses a new problem for the shipping industry
events.description: As the global economy focuses on the Strait of Hormuz crisis, another shipping crisis looms in the region -- the return of Somali pirates.

events.title: Crises in Amhara, Tigray cloud Ethiopia's election 2026
events.description: The June 1 elections come as the country debates federalism, governance and national unity in polls that could define the country's democratic future.

events.title: Macron announces big investments at France's 'electrification team' meeting
events.description: French President Emmanuel Macron gave the country's plans to move away from fossil fuels another boost on Tuesday, announcing big private investments, including a €1 billion EV project from Stellantis. Some 200 companies signed onto a national pact to join the government's efforts to double the share of electricity in the country's energy mix to 60 percent by 2030. Plus, BP has fired its chairman Albert Manifold over "serious concerns" about governance standards and conduct.

events.title: Starmer and Tusk sign UK–Poland defence pact amid warnings over Russian threats
events.description: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer and his Polish counterpart Donald Tusk have signed a new defence pact aimed at deepening military and security cooperation between the two NATO allies. The agreement focuses on strengthening joint capabilities, cyber defence and coordination in response to growing concerns over Russian aggression across Europe.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Starmer and Tusk sign UK–Poland defence pact amid warnings over Russian threats

events.title: North Korea says it tested new warheads, technology and navigation in latest launches
events.description: North Korea says its latest launches involved multiple weapons systems, including a nuclear-capable cruise missile that leader Kim Jong Un plans to deploy with front-line units facing South Korea
events.ticker_label: 🪖 North Korea says it tested new warheads, technology and navigation in latest launches

events.title: Heavy Israeli strikes hit southern Lebanon after large-scale evacuation orders
events.description: The Israeli military has ordered Lebanese residents to leave some 17% of the country's territory.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Heavy Israeli strikes hit southern Lebanon after large-scale evacuation orders

events.title: Iran war live: US strikes Iranian site; Kuwait targeted by missiles, drones
events.description: Kuwait's military says air defences are battling 'hostile' missiles and drones, as alarms sound across the country.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war live: US strikes Iranian site; Kuwait targeted by missiles, drones

events.title: President Sheinbaum allows Iran team to stay in Mexico during World Cup after US refusal
events.description: Fifa approached Mexico after US declined to host Iran squad despite it playing group games in the United States Mexico’s president, Claudia Sheinbaum , said on Monday her government agreed to allow the Iranian national football team to stay in Mexico during the World Cup , adding that the US did not want to host the team. The team will still play its group stage matches in the US but its base has been moved to Tijuana, Mexico, just south of San Diego, California, a move that Iran’s football federation announced recently and that was confirmed by Fifa, the sport’s governing body. Continue reading...

events.title: AI leaders soften warnings on job losses as industry reassesses impact
events.description: AI leaders including Jensen Huang and Sam Altman are moderating earlier warnings about mass job losses from artificial intelligence, suggesting that previous “doomsday” predictions overstated the near-term impact on employment. The shift comes as public concern over workplace disruption from AI continues to grow across major economies.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 AI leaders soften warnings on job losses as industry reassesses impact

events.title: Iran says it targeted American base after fresh US strikes
events.description: The hostilities come during a fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran, and protracted negotiations to end the three-month war.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran says it targeted American base after fresh US strikes

events.title: Oil prices jump after US launches new attacks on Iran
events.description: The strikes come despite a ceasefire between Tehran and Washington as the two countries hold peace talks.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Oil prices jump after US launches new attacks on Iran

events.title: Israeli attack on Gaza City kills at least 10, including four children
events.description: More than 20 others were injured in the attack, which came hours after the funeral of the head of Hamas’s armed wing.

events.title: US returns Palestinian rights expert Francesca Albanese to sanctions list
events.description: Trump administration has sought to pressure international officials who scrutinise reported abuses by Israeli forces.

events.title: Ebola: Uganda closes border with DR Congo as virus spreads
events.description: Uganda has shut its border "with immediate effect," despite warnings by the WHO that the move could backfire and cause the disease to spread.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ebola: Uganda closes border with DR Congo as virus spreads

events.title: Global temperatures likely to remain at record levels in 2026-2030, UN says
events.description: The UN warned Thursday that global average temperatures would be likely to stay at or near record levels for the 2026-2030 period, with a 75 percent chance that the average temperature for these five years will exceed pre-industrial levels by more than 1.5C.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Global temperatures likely to remain at record levels in 2026-2030, UN says

events.title: Middle East war live: US carries out strikes in Iran targeting military site
events.description: The US military on Thursday conducted its second defensive strike this week against Iran after officials said they observed aggressive activity from the Iranian military. This comes shortly after US President Donald Trump threatened to "finish the job" if Tehran didn't agree to a deal. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Middle East war live: US carries out strikes in Iran targeting military site

events.title: Khalida Popal and Javier Zanetti among football stars appointed UN Champions
events.description: With the World Cup just days away, 16 greats of the game have been appointed Football for the Goals (FFTG) Champions, using their profiles to raise awareness of the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) and advocate for a better future for people and the planet.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Khalida Popal and Javier Zanetti among football stars appointed UN Champions

events.title: Ebola-hit DR Congo faces 'catastrophic collision' of disease and conflict, WHO warns
events.description: Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus said fighting in DR Congo was hampering efforts to stop spread
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ebola-hit DR Congo faces 'catastrophic collision' of disease and conflict, WHO warns

events.title: ‘Makes no sense’: experts doubt pause in US arms sale to Taiwan is due to Iran war
events.description: While approval is due soon for $14bn deal, actual deliveries to Taiwan are years away – making ‘Operation Epic Fury’ in the Gulf an unlikely cause The Trump administration’s war against Iran should have no impact on arms sales to Taiwan, experts have said, after a US official suggested a pause in the delivery of a key weapons package was due to the Gulf conflict. Analysts told the Guardian that a $14bn arms package left in limbo after Donald Trump’s meeting with Xi Jinping could take up to six years to process, and there was a “low likelihood” of any true connection between events in Iran and weapons delivery to Taiwan. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘Makes no sense’: experts doubt pause in US arms sale to Taiwan is due to Iran war

events.title: Prospects fade for imminent end to Iran war as attacks restart
events.description: Israel says it will intensify attacks against Hezbollah in Lebanon and U.S. military struck Iranian boats and missile launch sites as envoys continued negotiations for a deal that would end the three-month war.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Prospects fade for imminent end to Iran war as attacks restart

events.title: Gaza: Life-saving medicines blocked as killing continues, disease gains ground
events.description: Dire conditions in Gaza marked by continuing violence, rodent infestations and the spread of infectious disease are being made worse by blockages of essential medical supplies, UN agencies warned on Friday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Gaza: Life-saving medicines blocked as killing continues, disease gains ground

events.title: Crash involving speeding train, minibus in Belgium leaves 4 dead including 2 children
events.description: Four people, including two children, were killed when a passenger train hit a minibus at a crossing near Buggenhout, Belgium, during the morning rush.

events.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
events.description: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

events.title: US judge dismisses criminal indictment against Kilmar Ábrego García
events.description: Judge said Trump administration would not have prosecuted Ábrego had he not challenged his high-profile deportation Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email A US judge dismissed a criminal indictment against Kilmar Ábrego García on Friday, finding that the Trump administration would not have prosecuted him had he not challenged his high-profile deportation. Ábrego, who had entered the United States without authorization in the past, became a symbol of the Trump administration’s drive for mass deportations when he was sent to the notorious anti-terrorism mega-prison in El Salvador known as Cecot in March 2025. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 US judge dismisses criminal indictment against Kilmar Ábrego García

events.title: Ghanaians repatriated from South Africa after anti-immigrant protests
events.description: Almost 300 Ghanaian nationals returned home on Wednesday after waves of anti-immigrant protests in South Africa heightened safety concerns. The group, which included children, boarded a flight from Johannesburg to Accra in what ​authorities described as ‌a voluntary repatriation process for Ghanaian citizens who say they no longer feel safe. FRANCE 24's Justice Baidoo reports.

events.title: ICC trial for ex-Philippine President Duterte to start in November
events.description: Judges at the International Criminal Court have set Nov. 30 as the start date for the trial of former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte for crimes against humanity

events.title: Middle East update: Airstrikes and evacuation orders in Lebanon, aid restrictions in Gaza
events.description: Intensified Israeli airstrikes overnight in Lebanon forced people to again flee their homes, while humanitarians in the Gaza Strip report continued restrictions in bringing aid into the enclave, the United Nations said on Tuesday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Middle East update: Airstrikes and evacuation orders in Lebanon, aid restrictions in Gaza

events.title: Israeli strike in Gaza City kills new head of Hamas's military wing
events.description: Hamas says Mohammed Odeh was killed alongside his wife and two children in a strike on a residential building.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Israeli strike in Gaza City kills new head of Hamas's military wing

events.title: Samsung memory chip staff in line for £310,000 bonuses after AI profit-sharing deal
events.description: Agreement averts strike and shows latest impact of AI boom as two more chipmakers join $1tn club Employees at Samsung Electronics’ memory chip division are to receive bonuses averaging about £310,000 each through a landmark profit-sharing agreement, as the AI boom drives up chipmakers’ profits. Fears of a strike at Samsung were averted on Wednesday after two unions for the world’s largest memory chipmaker said 74% of the 62,616 workers who cast their votes had backed the deal. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Samsung memory chip staff in line for £310,000 bonuses after AI profit-sharing deal

events.title: France moves towards symbolic repealing of slavery legislation
events.description: France is Thursday expected to move towards repealing outdated legislation that defines people enslaved in its colonies as "moveable goods", in a symbolic move as the country grapples with its colonial legacy. The French were the third largest slave traders in Europe, after the British and the Portuguese. France abolished enslaving humans more than 170 years ago, and in 2001 recognised slavery and the slave trade as "crimes against humanity".
events.ticker_label: 🪖 France moves towards symbolic repealing of slavery legislation

events.title: Inside online rape networks
events.description: Gisèle Pelicot has become a household name since the world learned of the unimaginable ordeal she suffered at the hands of her husband. What many of us might be shocked to discover is that there are still many websites where men exchange tips and ideas on how to drug women so they can rape them in their sleep. That is the subject of a new documentary made by CNN's Saskya Vandoorne. She joined us on Entre Nous to talk about her investigation.

events.title: Fire at girls' boarding school in Kenya kills 16

events.title: US firm fined for Australian immigration security failures. How much? Border force refuses to say

events.title: Why the French Open is named after Roland Garros, who didn't play tennis

events.title: France moves to repeal yet-to-be-abolished slavery law, Code Noir
events.description: French lawmakers are set to vote on Thursday to repeal the Code Noir, a law that regulated slavery in the French colonies which was never formally repealed even after the abolition of slavery in 1848. The vote reopens a debate around reparations in a country that has yet to fully come to terms with its history of colonialism.

events.title: Trump endorses Armenian Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan ahead of tense June poll
events.description: US President Donald Trump announced on Wednesday that he would support Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan in Armenia’s June 7 parliamentary elections, a vote that serves as a litmus test for the government of the South Caucasus nation which has recently shifted its alignment toward the West and distanced itself from Russia.

events.title: ‘She does not back down’: the couple seeking to legalise same-sex marriage in Botswana
events.description: Bonolo Selelo and Tsholofelo Kumile are going to court for right to wed but face fierce opposition from church groups Bonolo Selelo was at Botswana’s national museum for a Gaborone Pride event when she spotted Tsholofelo Kumile and was struck by her good looks. The two initiated a conversation and when Kumile expressed anxiety about what a tarot reading at the event might hold, Selelo thought nothing of offering her a hug. The reading turned out positive but Kumile claimed her hug anyway and they talked for hours. That was 1 October 2023. Two months later, they moved in together. Then, on a hike during the Easter holidays in 2024, Selelo proposed to Kumile. A year later, they visited a local government office to register their intent to marry and were told it wasn’t legal. Continue reading...

events.title: Silicon Valley Bank collapse renews calls to address disparities impacting entrepreneurs of color

events.title: Reuters executive Simon Robinson to replace Justin Stevens as ABC news director

events.title: Greener pasture of a shepherd’s life lures Chinese workers penned in by ‘996’ jobs
events.description: When an Inner Mongolia farmer sought two herders to tend his 3,000 sheep, he was swamped with applicants including graduates, factory labourers and white-collar workers A Chinese farm owner’s recruitment drive for shepherds has ended in success after his job advert seeking people to work on his Inner Mongolia ranch went viral, drawing the attention of city dwellers struggling to find work and highlighting growing strains in China’s labour market. Zuo Xiaoyong posted an advert on Chinese social media in late April seeking two shepherds, preferably a couple, to take 3,000 sheep out to graze on a 2,000ha pasture in the summer. The shepherds would also undertake indoor feeding and cleaning during the winter when temperatures can drop below -30C at his ranch roughly 300km from Xilinhot city, near the Mongolian border. Continue reading...

events.title: Victorian greyhound trainer avoids ban despite dog being found with cocaine in system
events.description: Tribunal decision on Geoffrey Dalton comes as advocacy group releases data showing 30 greyhounds have died at or after Victorian races this year Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A veteran greyhound trainer whose dog was found to have cocaine in its system at a race has avoided a temporary ban despite the industry regulator calling for one. The Victorian Civil and Administrative Tribunal (Vcat) on Thursday decided not to impose tougher penalties on Geoffrey Dalton, who was fined $300 after the dog tested positive for cocaine before a 2024 race. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Victorian greyhound trainer avoids ban despite dog being found with cocaine in system

events.title: Treasury secretary says ‘no clear evidence’ for CGT reform criticisms – as it happened
events.description: This blog is now closed Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Scams come in all shapes and sizes (and none of them are nice), and the government is considering creating rules that would force banks, telcos and digital platforms to automatically reimburse victims of smaller scams of up to $3,000. Labor is considering a range of options as part of a scam protection framework. For smaller losses, $3,000 and under, what we’re proposing is that there should be automatic payment to consumers where they can verify that there has been a scam. Scams that get into the six figures: some investment scams, some romance scams. And that’s where dispute resolution processes would come into play. What we want to do is to make sure that we don’t have the wrong incentives for perpetrators to see Australia as a soft target. But the balance is that with very small claims we don’t want to have processes that are completely disproportionate to the value of the sum in dispute. Taxpayer funds should never be used for private business, nor were they in this case. When we’re undertaking whether it be community engagement, media engagement, stakeholder meetings, you know, assisting colleagues, there are arrangements in place to assist us to do that work that is right and appropriate, and it is not right and appropriate to bill the taxpayer, as you say, for personal activities. And I did not do that. Yep. Continue reading...

events.title: In Colombia, a flashy lawyer touts his tough-on-crime approach in presidential race
events.description: In Colombia's election this Sunday, a flamboyant criminal lawyer — who flaunts his wealth, machismo and even his music videos — is pitching himself as the toughest candidate on crime.

events.title: New Ebola cases in Congo: What you need to know
events.description: The virus spreads from direct person-to-person contact. But here's what makes it especially lethal: it persists in corpses, and funeral practices often take place precisely when bodies are most infectious.

events.title: Turkey: Leadership dispute engulfs opposition CHP
events.description: The top figures in Turkey's main opposition party differ on how to resolve the leadership vacuum after a court annulled its 2023 primary. One wants the party delegates to pick a leader, the other wants members to vote.

events.title: A rich man’s game? How Roland Garros host France turned its back on clay
events.description: Efforts to expand tennis in France have seen hard courts gradually replace clay in the land of Roland Garros, leaving the iconic slippery surface to a select few – and, according to some, contributing to the home players’ miserable run at the Paris Grand Slam tournament.

events.title: Fake Pride, masked men and a surge in misinformation ahead of Armenia’s election
events.description: There has been a “tenfold increase” since the start of May in misinformation circulating online in Armenia ahead of the June 7 parliamentary elections, according to Artur Papyan, the director of Armenia’s Media Diversity Institute. Both the ruling party and the opposition – as well as foreign interests – have all been accused of playing a part in this surge, which could have long-lasting consequences for Armenia's democracy.

events.title: Germany's most-wanted woman, 67, jailed after decades on run
events.description: A former member of the notorious Baader-Meinhof gang has been jailed for a string of armed robberies after decades on the run.

events.title: Spanish police search prime minister's party HQ over corruption allegations
events.description: Police have searched the Madrid headquarters of Spain's ruling Socialist Party over a potential illegal payments scheme.

events.title: Viral 'Donald Trump' buffalo spared from sacrifice in Bangladesh
events.description: A viral buffalo nicknamed "Donald Trump" for its blond tuft has been spared from Eid al-Adha sacrifice after a last-minute government intervention.

events.title: Muslim pilgrims perform Hajj rituals in strong heat as Eid al-Adha celebrations start
events.description: Pilgrims in Saudi Arabia threw pebbles at a large pillar in a symbolic ritual during the final days of Hajj

events.title: Northern Ireland's former unionist leader faces trial in sexual abuse case involving 2 girls
events.description: The former leader of Northern Ireland’s Democratic Unionist Party is on trial for allegedly sexually abusing two girls

events.description: The Organization for the Prohibition of Chemical Weapons says it has found dozens of undeclared chemical bombs and rockets in Syria

events.title: Brazil is set to join other Latin American countries with a 40-hour, 5-day workweek
events.description: Brazil was set to join other Latin American countries that have shortened working hours as the lower house approved a constitutional amendment establishing a 40-hour, five-day workweek

events.title: Brazil to invest $75 million in highway through Amazon and unveils environmental protection plan
events.description: Brazil’s government has announced a $75 million investment in the BR-319 highway, a move environmentalists fear could speed up Amazon deforestation

events.description: Colombia is set to elect a new president and vice president on May 31

events.title: California teacher Samantha Watson arrested for alleged sexual conduct with student at charter school
events.description: California teacher Samantha Watson allegedly had inappropriate sexual contact with a minor student. Investigators believe there may be more victims.

events.title: Feds seize $40M in gold bars, cash, Rolexes from former CIA official who faked being a Navy pilot
events.description: A former senior U.S. official with top secret clearance was allegedly caught with $40 million in gold bars after an FBI raid on his Virginia home.

events.title: ‘Golden boy’ Ivy League grad who murdered hedge-fund dad over allowance speaks out in new interview
events.description: Convicted killer Thomas Gilbert Jr. breaks silence in prison interview, bizarrely claiming innocence in his hedge-fund father's 2015 Manhattan murder.

events.title: Video shows illegal aliens found crammed inside semi-truck sleeper cab in alleged smuggling bust
events.description: Texas DPS released video of a human smuggling bust where 20 illegal aliens were discovered packed inside a semi-truck during a traffic stop.

events.title: Israeli ambassador compares France's far-left leader's rhetoric to Hitler as antisemitism surges
events.description: France recorded 1,320 antisemitic acts in 2025, up from 436 in 2022, as Jewish families alter daily habits to avoid being identified, CRIF's president says.

events.title: Mass tanker blackout rattles Gulf ahead of 1.35M-barrel oil transfer amid US-Iran talks: firm
events.description: Maritime tracking signals collapse near the UAE's Fujairah oil hub hours before President Trump announced progress on a bilateral peace deal with Iran, according to an AI maritime firm.
